Ms. Handy reported that subpoenaed bank records had been received from Wells Fargo relating to an account held in the decedent's name prior to death. Ms. Handy stated that additional accounts had been identified through the production and that the forensic accountant would need time to review those records and determine their significance. Ms. Handy advised that the forensic accountant anticipated completing the report within approximately 60 to 90 days. Ms. Handy also stated that an evidentiary hearing would likely be necessary following completion of the forensic accounting.

Following discussion, the Court continued all matters to April 27, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for completion of the forensic accounting and further case management. The Court directed the parties to file an updated joint case management statement addressing the status of the forensic accounting and the anticipated scope of any evidentiary hearing, to be filed one week prior to the continued hearing.

Petition For Dismissal Of Accounting Petition; Return Of Property; Removal Of Administration; Double Damages And Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Apr-21-2025 Continued To Jul-21-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Gary Stevens, in propria persona, former Administrator.

Amber Handy, Esq., for and with Marla Sarpy, Personal Representative.

William Whiteman, Esq., in propria persona, interested party.

Amber Kim, Esq., for U.S. Specialty Company, Requestor.

The court at the outset confirms with the parties that mediation was unsuccessful. There is discussion regarding Mr. Stevens' pending accountings and of the unsuccessful attempts to clear procedural issues. At the court's query, Ms. Handy requests an evidentiary hearing or trial and offers advocacy for the request stating that Mr. Stevens has had ample time to prepare accountings. After an additional query from the court, Ms. Handy offers her position regarding having her client prepare and file a forensic accounting stating that she and her client have discussed the preceding and will be ready to do file a forensic accounting should the court order one. The court advises that it is not inclined to order this matter to trial advising that it feels that this matter might be able to be resolved once a forensic accounting has been filed. Ms. Kim offers her position advising that her client would like to work with Mr. Stevens and proposes continuing this matter to allow time to do so. Mr. Stevens reports that he is actively seeking counsel to assist him with this matter.

After additional discussion, including a continuance date, the court orders as follows:

After additional discussion, the Accounting Status is continued to September 1, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.
